edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:d5af1389c17b
edges.original_sentence: Dams associated with hydropower production or water management can serve as barriers to ﬁsh migration (Lucas and Baras, 2001), reducing watershed connectivity and resulting in changes to ﬁsh community structure and system productivity (Sheer and Steel, 2006).

source_spans.text: Dams associated with hydropower production or water management can serve as barriers to ﬁsh migration (Lucas and Baras, 2001), reducing watershed connectivity and resulting in changes to ﬁsh community structure and system productivity (Sheer and Steel, 2006).

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:f8c01f5e792e
edges.original_sentence: Pratt et al. (2009) attributed differences in attraction and passage ef ﬁciency between white sucker and rock bass (Ambloplites rupestris )a ta ﬁshway to motivation, with comparatively poor passage of the latter species attributed to its facultative migratory tendency.

source_spans.text: Pratt et al. (2009) attributed differences in attraction and passage ef ﬁciency between white sucker and rock bass (Ambloplites rupestris )a ta ﬁshway to motivation, with comparatively poor passage of the latter species attributed to its facultative migratory tendency.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:c6482a9e0d94
edges.original_sentence: Interestingly, time of day that ﬁshway entry occurs can also affect passage behaviour. For example, White et al. (2011) found that bony herring ( Nematalosa erebi) entering the ﬁshway during the day passed uninterrupted; however, upon entry in the evening, the same species delayed its passage overnight.

source_spans.text: Interestingly, time of day that ﬁshway entry occurs can also affect passage behaviour. For example, White et al. (2011) found that bony herring ( Nematalosa erebi) entering the ﬁshway during the day passed uninterrupted; however, upon entry in the evening, the same species delayed its passage overnight.

source_document.title: MULTISPECIES FISH PASSAGE BEHAVIOUR IN A VERTICAL SLOT FISHWAY ON THE RICHELIEU RIVER, QUEBEC, CANADA
